item.title: The Surrealist Blues Poet aja monet’s Jazzy New Album
item.description: Also: Joan Semmel’s revolutionary nudes, Aleshea Harris’s film adaptation of “Is God Is,” Rachel Syme on thrift markets galore, and more.

item.title: Can the Democrats Take Back the Senate?
item.description: Their electoral prospects are finally improving, but opportunities can quickly give way to divisions. Does the Party have a plan?
item.dc:creator: ByAmy Davidson Sorkin

item.title: Shaggy’s Boombastic Pilates Session
item.description: In town to talk about his new album, “Lottery,” and en route to a collaboration with Sting, the Grammy-winning reggae star stretches and shakes at a reformer workout class.
item.dc:creator: ByNatalie Meade

item.title: Can Mozart and Salieri Work It Out?
item.description: At the Morgan Library’s Mozart exhibition, Will Sharpe and Paul Bettany dish about playing classical music’s most notorious rivals, on Starz’s new “Amadeus” reboot.
item.dc:creator: ByMichael Schulman

item.title: Baseball’s Magna Carta Finds a New Home
item.description: In 1999, Frank Murray bid in a Sotheby’s auction and nabbed the “Laws of Base Ball,” a nineteenth-century document detailing early rules of America’s pastime. Now it’s worth two hundred and fifty times what he paid.
item.dc:creator: ByBen McGrath

item.title: Where the Met Gala Really Begins
item.description: Every year, the Mark Hotel is transformed into a chaotic celebrity holding pen.
item.dc:creator: ByJane Bua

item.title: The Human-Trafficking Victim Next Door
item.description: A young girl was brought from Guinea to a wealthy suburb near Dallas. She spent the next sixteen years of her life in forced servitude.
item.dc:creator: ByYudhijit Bhattacharjee

item.title: The Mystery and Mass Appeal of the N.F.L. Draft
item.description: The annual draft is watched by millions, and analyzed by a set of professional and amateur prognosticators. Still, it comes down to a crapshoot.
item.dc:creator: ByDan Greene

item.title: Can Hakeem Jeffries Lead a Democratic Takeover of the House?
item.description: An unprecedented gerrymandering effort led by Donald Trump—and internal divisions among Democrats—has made the Minority Leader’s path to victory harder than ever.
item.dc:creator: ByJason Zengerle

item.title: Boots Riley, Marx Brother
item.description: Boots Riley’s zany movies combine pop aesthetics with radical politics.
item.dc:creator: ByEmily Nussbaum

item.title: Realistic High-School-Yearbook Inscriptions
item.description: “You will read this once this afternoon and not again until you’re in your fifties, but, by that time, I will have already died, alone in my apartment, totally nude, except for my ankle monitor.”
item.dc:creator: ByJason RoederandMike Sacks

item.title: “The Dreamdrive”
item.description: Each morning, he “awoke”—not the term he would have used—exhausted, having not slept and having driven all night.
item.dc:creator: ByWeike Wang

item.title: The Prehistory of A.I. Slop
item.description: Before ChatGPT, there was the Plot Robot, Auto-Beatnik, and a century’s worth of schemes for automating authorship.
item.dc:creator: ByJill Lepore

item.title: Mary Todd Lincoln Has Long Been Derided. Is Her Reputation Salvageable?
item.description: History knows the First Lady as a hysterical widow and a lavish spender. Her most recent biographer chooses to highlight her mental fortitude and political prowess.
item.dc:creator: ByThomas Mallon

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “Into the Wood Chipper,” “Transcendence for Beginners,” “Paradiso 17,” and “The Monuments of Paris.”

item.title: The Age of “Intentional” Drinking
item.description: Americans are losing their appetite for booze. Could the mini Martini lure them back?
item.dc:creator: ByHannah Goldfield

item.title: Rostam Batmanglij Wanders to the Edges of American Sound
item.description: The polymath musician, formerly of Vampire Weekend, likes to push our idea of what a pop song can be.
item.dc:creator: ByAmanda Petrusich

item.title: Chaya Czernowin Gives Voice to a Wounded World
item.description: The composer’s work, featured at a recent festival in Germany, includes a howling denunciation of war crimes against children.
item.dc:creator: ByAlex Ross

item.title: For/Against Summer, a Spell
item.description: “In sorrowship dues, in sap, on the spines of pines, / despite the winter of hearts, summer came.”
item.dc:creator: ByValzhyna Mort

item.title: “The Angel of Retail”
item.description: “Your spirit of grace in the taffy machine’s chrome arms / At Morris’s Candy performing a sarabande / Unknitting and knitting again immaculate sweets.”
item.dc:creator: ByRobert Pinsky

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the May 25, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Tuesday, May 12, 2026
item.description: A moderately challenging puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByWyna Liu
